nativeRange.habitat: Coral-rich areas of lagoon and seaward reefs, at depths of 2–23 m; often found among seagrass beds and rubble zones

diet.notes: Has been documented consuming microalgae (spirulina), small crustaceans (mysis, brine shrimp, krill), zooplankton, and pelagic tunicates. In aquaria, generally advised to offer a variety of meaty frozen foods and vegetable-based preparations such as spirulina flakes.

summary: A striking damselfish that displays vivid yellow and black juvenile coloration, though adults tend to fade to brown and black. Generally considered reef-safe but have been known to become highly aggressive as they mature.
careNotes:
Juvenile Neoglyphidodon nigroris display bright yellow coloration with two horizontal black bars, making them appealing for reef displays; however, coloration typically fades to brown and black with maturity. Adults have been known to become extremely aggressive toward tankmates, which can make selecting compatible fish challenging. Generally considered reef-safe and have not been reported to nip corals or bother invertebrates.

Sources disagreed on minimum tank size: LiveAquaria recommended 30 gallons (~114 L), SaltwaterFish.com listed 20 gallons (~76 L), and ReefApp suggested 300 L (~79 gallons). The average of these recommendations is approximately 163 L; given the wide spread (76–300 L), this field has been flagged for review. A tank of at least 113–150 L is commonly suggested, with larger volumes advisable given the species' territorial nature as adults.

Temperature data also showed some spread across sources: LiveAquaria cited 72–78°F (22–26°C), TankFacts cited 76–80°F (24–27°C), and FishBase wild-range data indicated 25–29°C. The averaged range of approximately 24–27°C is used here. FishBase records a maximum total length of 13.0 cm; aquarium sources typically cite 5 inches (12.7 cm). Typical aquarium specimens tend to reach 8–10 cm.

SaltwaterFish.com listed the species under the synonym Paraglyphidodon nigroris; the accepted name per FishBase (2026) is Neoglyphidodon nigroris (Cuvier, 1830). Some sources also list this species as 'semi-aggressive' while others call it 'aggressive'; the species appears to be manageable when young but tends toward significant aggression at adult size, so 'aggressive' is used here.

Breeding in captivity has been documented; eggs are laid on substrate and guarded by the male. The species is oviparous with distinct pair bonding during spawning.
breedingNotes: Reported to be oviparous with distinct pair bonding during breeding; eggs adhere to a substrate surface and are guarded and aerated by the male. Breeding in home aquaria has been reported but is considered uncommon. Specific captive-breeding difficulty data was not available from consulted sources.

additional.name: SaltwaterFish.com
additional.notes: Cross-checked sizing, tank size, temperament, diet, and reef-safe status. Lists species under synonym Paraglyphidodon nigroris; listed care level as Easy and temperament as Semi-Aggressive. Tank minimum listed as 20 gallons.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Used for taxonomic confirmation, native range, maximum size (13.0 cm TL), temperature range (25.2–29.3°C), and habitat description. FishBase confirms Neoglyphidodon nigroris as accepted name.

additional.name: ReefApp Species Lexicon
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (13 cm), salinity (SG 1.026), minimum tank (300 L), temperament (aggressive/territorial), and reef-safe status. Provided alternate common names.

additional.name: TankFacts
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature (76–80°F / 24.4–26.7°C), pH (7.8–8.4), salinity (SG 1.023–1.026), and dKH (8–12).

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous with distinct pair bonding; eggs attached to substrate and guarded by male. Rarely bred in captivity.
